hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The response encoding is 'gzip' because the client's Accept-Encoding header includes 'gzip' as the first matching candidate in the configuration list.

context_pointers.vague_reference: See how aliases are handled in the header mapping logic
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The output format for the alias action maps strictly to the alias key (e.g., 'name2') and its value, rather than the origin key.

context_pointers.vague_reference: Check the JSON serialization escape rules applied to unicode strings
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Bytes with encoding chunks are outputted as '{\n' followed by a newline character in the literal string.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_request_defaults_and_options..description: A request object exposes default HTTP request state and copies supplied route, URL, header, body, query, file, cookie, and custom metadata into observable fields.

feature1_2_request_header_lookup.json.description: Request header lookup is case-insensitive and treats the common referrer spelling variants as equivalent; missing headers produce an absent value.

cases.expected_output:
lookup_KEY1=value1
alias_KEY1=value1
lookup_key2=value2
alias_key2=value2
lookup_missing=absent
alias_missing=absent

feature1_3_request_content_negotiation.j.description: Request content negotiation reports the first acceptable media type, encoding, charset, or language from a candidate list and returns false when none match.

cases.expected_output:
match_0=html
match_1=false
match_2=html
match_3=gzip
match_4=utf-8
match_5=en-GB

feature1_4_request_range_parsing.json.description: A request range parser returns an absent result for absent ranges, negative sentinel values for invalid or unsatisfiable ranges, and inclusive byte ranges for valid headers with optional combining.

cases.expected_output: range=absent
range=-1

feature1_5_request_parameter_resolution..description: Request parameter lookup checks route parameters before body fields before query fields, preserves falsy values, and returns a caller-provided fallback when absent.

cases.expected_output: value=from-route
value=0

feature1_6_request_stream_and_host_helpe.description: A mock request can emit supplied body payloads as stream data and derives hostname and subdomain information from the host header unless an explicit hostname is provided.

cases.expected_output:
data=test data
ended=true

data={"key":"value"}
ended=true

feature2_1_response_initial_state_and_co.description: A response object starts with success status, empty cookies, and supplied local variables; cookie operations store values with options and clearing a cookie writes an expired empty cookie.

cases.expected_output:
status=200
status_message=OK
headers={}
cookies={"name":{"options":absent,"value":"value"}}
data=
buffer=
encoding=absent
ended=false
headers_sent=false
writable_finished=false
data_length_valid=true

feature2_2_response_status_and_body_seri.description: Response status and body operations update status code, content type, serialized data, encoding, completion flags, and emitted body data for text, status messages, JSON values, and JSONP values.

cases.expected_output:
status=404
status_message=OK
headers={}
cookies={}
data=
buffer=
encoding=absent
ended=false
headers_sent=false
writable_finished=false
data_length_valid=true

status=404
status_message=OK
headers={"content-type":"text/plain"}
cookies={}
data=Not Found
buffer=
encoding=absent
ended=true
headers_sent=true
writable_finished=true
data_length_valid=true

status=200
status_message=OK
headers={}
cookies={}
data=payload
buffer=
encoding=absent
ended=true
headers_sent=true
writable_finished=true
data_length_valid=true

feature2_3_response_header_management.js.description: Response header management supports MIME type resolution, direct and bulk setting, appending repeated values, Vary de-duplication, case-insensitive reads, names listing, presence checks, removal, and location headers.

cases.expected_output:
status=200
status_message=OK
headers={"content-type":"text/plain"}
cookies={}
data=
buffer=
encoding=absent
ended=false
headers_sent=false
writable_finished=false
data_length_valid=true
read_Content-Type=text/plain

status=200
status_message=OK
headers={"bool":false,"name1":"value1","name2":"value2","name3":"value3","num":1}
cookies={}
data=
buffer=
encoding=absent
ended=false
headers_sent=false
writable_finished=false
data_length_valid=true
read_name1=value1
read_name2=value2
read_name3=value3
read_num=1
read_bool=false

status=200
status_message=OK
headers={"link":["<http://localhost/>","<http://localhost:80/>"]}
cookies={}
data=
buffer=
encoding=absent
ended=false
headers_sent=false
writable_finished=false
data_length_valid=true
read_Link=["<http://localhost/>","<http://localhost:80/>"]

feature2_4_response_write_end_and_head.j.description: Low-level response writing concatenates string and buffer payloads, tracks encoding and finish state, writes status line and headers before body transmission, and preserves already-sent headers from later status writes.

cases.expected_output:
status=200
status_message=OK
headers={}
cookies={}
data=payload1payload2
buffer=
encoding=utf8
ended=false
headers_sent=true
writable_finished=false
data_length_valid=true

status=200
status_message=OK
headers={}
cookies={}
data=
buffer=payload1payload2
encoding=absent
ended=true
headers_sent=true
writable_finished=true
data_length_valid=true

feature2_5_response_redirect_render_and_.description: Higher-level response helpers capture redirect targets, rendered view names and data, callback rendering results, accepted response-format handlers, and 406 not-acceptable fallbacks.

cases.expected_output:
status=302
redirect_url=/path/to/redirect
render_view=
render_data={}
events=["end","finish"]

status=301
redirect_url=/path/to/redirect
render_view=
render_data={}
events=["end","finish"]

status=200
redirect_url=
render_view=view
render_data={}
events=["render","end","finish"]

_constraints.input_condition: Object creation with scenario 'request_state' and no specific options provided.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns a snapshot of all default observable fields.

_constraints.input_condition: Header lookup where the requested name has mixed casing compared to stored key.
_constraints.output_rule: Lookup must be case-insensitive (treats 'referer' and 'referrer' as equivalent).

_constraints.input_condition: Header lookup for a key that does not exist in the supplied headers dictionary.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 'absent' for both prefixed and alias variations.

_constraints.input_condition: Content Negotiation check with 'kind' set to 'body_type' and 'values' containing multiple items.
_constraints.output_rule: Reports 'false' if the current body type (e.g., 'text/html') does not match the candidate list (e.g., ['json', 'html', 'text']). Matches first compatible.

_constraints.input_condition: Content Negotiation check with 'kind' set to 'body_type' and 'values' containing a single item that mismatches the body.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 'false' immediately if no candidate matches.

_constraints.input_condition: Content Negotiation check with 'kind' set to 'accept'.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns the first value in the 'values' list that matches an 'accept' header. If none match, returns 'false'.

_constraints.input_condition: Content Negotiation check with 'kind' set to 'encoding' against 'Accept-Encoding' header.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns the first matching encoding (case insensitive) from the candidate list.

_constraints.input_condition: Content Negotiation check with 'kind' set to 'charset' against 'Accept-Charset' header.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns the first matching charset.

_constraints.input_condition: Content Negotiation check with 'kind' set to 'language' against 'Accept-Language' header.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns the first matching language tag.

_constraints.input_condition: Request Range parsing headers present but unsatisfiable or malformed (e.g., 'bytes=90-100' on a size 10 resource).
_constraints.output_rule: Returns a negative sentinel value.

_constraints.input_condition: Request Range parsing headers absent or empty.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 'absent'.

_constraints.input_condition: Parameter resolution with route params, body, and query providing values.
_constraints.output_rule: Route parameters take precedence over body and query.

_constraints.input_condition: Parameter resolution where the target value is explicitly 0 in the body.
_constraints.output_rule: Must preserve the literal 0 value, not treat it as falsy/absent.

_constraints.input_condition: Parameter resolution using a fallback defaultValue when no route, body, or query value exists.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns the caller-provided fallback value.

_constraints.input_condition: Body stream operation with payload value.
_constraints.output_rule: Emits the data string followed by 'ended=true' flag.

_constraints.input_condition: Body string 'test data' passed as payload kind 'value'.
_constraints.output_rule: Data output matches input string exactly.

_constraints.input_condition: Structure object passed as payload value.
_constraints.output_rule: JSON serializes the object value.

_constraints.input_condition: Response object creation with NO actions specified.
_constraints.output_rule: Status is 200, cookies {}, locals {}, data empty, ended false, headers_sent false.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'store_cookie' with name and value.
_constraints.output_rule: Adds entry to cookies dict structure.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'set_status_code' (e.g., 404).
_constraints.output_rule: Updates status code. Status message remains 'OK' unless send_status_message is called. Status message does not change on status code set alone.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'send_status_message' with code (e.g., 404).
_constraints.output_rule: Updates status, sets content-type to 'text/plain', sets data to the message (e.g., 'Not Found'), sets ended=true, headers_sent=true.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'send_body' with value kind.
_constraints.output_rule: Sets content-type to 'application/json' (inferred from value kind), ends response.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'assign_header_alias' with name2 alias for value2.
_constraints.output_rule: Stores alias name2 and its value separately or maps alias to original name. In output format `assign_header_alias` maps to the specific alias name.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'add_header_value' with repeated 'Link' header.
_constraints.output_rule: Accumulates multiple values for the same header name.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'write_body_part' with buffer payload.
_constraints.output_rule: Stores data in 'buffer' key, does not change 'data'. 'ended' remains false unless explicitly finished.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'finish_body' with payload.
_constraints.output_rule: Moves payload to 'data'. Sets 'ended=true' and 'writable_finished=true'.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'redirect_to'.
_constraints.output_rule: Sets status to 302 (unless code is 301), sets redirect_url, delays 'render_view'.

_constraints.input_condition: Redirect to with explicit 301 code.
_constraints.output_rule: Sets status to 301.

_constraints.input_condition: Action: 'render_view' with view name.
_constraints.output_rule: Sets render_view and render_data (if data passed), sets events.

_constraints.input_condition: Final state after 'write_body_part' followed by 'finish_body'.
_constraints.output_rule: Concatenates strings across parts, then clears buffer into data.

_constraints.input_condition: Setting header via 'set_body_type', then bulk assign.
_constraints.output_rule: Echoes default Content-Type if set_body_type called without value, then overrides with bulk.

_constraints.input_condition: Header value contains special characters or unicode in body stream.
_constraints.output_rule: JSON escaping rules apply for object serialization, ASCII preserved for strings.

_constraints.input_condition: Check for header value absence (e.g., 'read_Content-Type' when content-type header is not set).
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 'content-type='.

_constraints.input_condition: Missing header lookup (e.g., 'KEY1' when only 'Key2' exists - though test case C002 implies case insensitivity).
_constraints.output_rule: Returns absent if no match found via case-insensitive lookup.

_constraints.input_condition: Request bodies of different types (JSON, HTML, Text).
_constraints.output_rule: Content Negotiation determines first match based on Accept headers.

_constraints.input_condition: Reading non-existent headers via 'readHeaders'.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns key with empty value or 'absent' suffix depending on implementation logic not shown in snippet but implied by 'read' prefix.

_constraints.input_condition: Coolies with expiration set in store_cookie action.
_constraints.output_rule: Stored with options:absent in output format of test case (inferred from C019).

_constraints.input_condition: Erasing a cookie (store_cookie with empty/null value or explicit remove action implied by 'clearing' description).
_constraints.output_rule: Write an expired empty cookie.

_constraints.input_condition: Range header value 'bytes=90-100' on size 10.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns -1 for unsatisfiable range.

_constraints.input_condition: Parsing actions list containing 'add_header_value'.
_constraints.output_rule: Appends to existing header list if present (Link header).

hide.replacement: a specific encoding preference (requires runtime header inspection)
